criteria.name: Identification of Constraints
criteria.description: Ability to identify and clearly state all relevant constraints in the problem.
criteria.excellent_indicator: All constraints are accurately identified and clearly articulated, including implicit constraints.
criteria.poor_indicator: Constraints are missing, incomplete, or incorrectly identified.

criteria.name: Objective Function Formulation
criteria.description: Ability to translate the problem into a well-defined objective function.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The objective function is correctly formulated and aligns with the problem's goals.
criteria.poor_indicator: The objective function is incorrectly formulated or does not match the problem's goals.

criteria.name: Understanding of Problem Context
criteria.description: Demonstrates understanding of the problem context and its implications for the optimization task.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Shows deep understanding of the problem context and correctly integrates it into the task framing.
criteria.poor_indicator: Lacks understanding of the problem context or misinterprets its implications.

typical_errors: Neglecting implicit constraints that affect the solution space.
Formulating an objective function that does not reflect the problem's goals.
Misinterpreting the problem context and its impact on constraints.

criteria.name: Error Identification in Calculations
criteria.description: Ability to identify errors in the AI's mathematical calculations related to constrained optimization.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Correctly identifies all errors in calculations, including subtle mistakes.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to identify significant errors or identifies incorrect errors.

criteria.name: Validation of Feasibility
criteria.description: Ability to assess whether the AI's solution respects all problem constraints.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately evaluates the feasibility of solutions with respect to all constraints.
criteria.poor_indicator: Overlooks constraint violations or incorrectly judges feasible solutions as infeasible.

criteria.name: Optimality Assessment
criteria.description: Ability to determine if the AI's solution is optimal given the constraints.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately assesses the optimality of solutions, considering constraint boundaries.
criteria.poor_indicator: Misjudges optimality or fails to recognize suboptimal solutions.

typical_errors: Overlooking errors in constraint handling in the AI's solution.
Misjudging the feasibility of solutions due to incorrect constraint interpretation.
Failing to recognize when a solution is suboptimal.

criteria.name: Effective Correction Commands
criteria.description: Ability to provide clear and effective correction commands to improve AI output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Commands are precise, addressing specific errors and guiding the AI towards a correct solution.
criteria.poor_indicator: Commands are vague, incorrect, or do not address the identified errors.

criteria.name: Guidance on Constraint Handling
criteria.description: Ability to direct the AI in correctly applying constraints to the optimization problem.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Provides clear, actionable guidance on constraint application, leading to correct constraint handling.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to provide effective guidance on constraints, leading to persistent errors.

criteria.name: Feedback on Objective Function Adjustments
criteria.description: Ability to guide adjustments to the objective function to better align with problem goals.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Feedback is specific and improves alignment of the objective function with problem goals.
criteria.poor_indicator: Feedback is irrelevant or does not improve the objective function alignment.

typical_errors: Providing correction commands that are too general or not actionable.
Failing to guide the AI in properly handling constraints.
Giving feedback that does not improve or misaligns the objective function.
